open_findings.title: Quoted command implementation prompts can be normalized into workflow execution routing when they contain workflow verbs.
open_findings.summary: The quoted-command guard treats any workflow marker anywhere in the prompt as invocation intent. Prompts such as `add tests for `/memory:save` run-mode guard` therefore bypass the implementation-reference guard and normalize to the owning skill.

open_findings.title: Runtime source-metadata health ignores the nested skill-advisor graph-metadata file that the compiler treats as authoritative.
open_findings.summary: The compiler discovers and fails on the nested advisor graph metadata, but the runtime health loaders only scan top-level skill folders. A corrupt nested advisor metadata file can therefore leave health_check source_metadata healthy.

open_findings.title: Regression coverage misses the quoted-command workflow-verb case and nested advisor metadata health case.
open_findings.summary: Existing tests cover top-level corrupt metadata and simple command-target guards, but not the two implementation edge cases found in this pass.
